title: davallia polypodioides var. hirta
intro: Davallia polypodioides var. hirta, also known as the Davallia fern or Hairy Davallia, is a variety of the Davallia polypodioides species, a type of fern native to tropical Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation, fever, and pain, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Davallia polypodioides var. hirta include its use as a treatment for wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in herbal preparations like tea, tincture, and salve, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
